questionText: For your electricity generation activities, provide details of your nameplate capacity and electricity generation specifics for each technology employed.
questionKo: For your electricity generation activities, 세부 정보를 입력 of your nameplate capacity 및 electricity generation specifics for each technology employed.
simpleHelp: Each power generation source is associated with specific dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities, requiring tailored management procedures and future planning. For example, hydropower will use and impact water resources in a different way to biomass power generation plants. Asking organizations to disclose their power generation sources provides data users with the contextual information necessary to understand and evaluate how they assess and manage climate- and water-related dependencies, impacts, risks, and opportunities. This also enables data users to make more accurate cross-organizational and cross-industry comparisons.

rows.title: Fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture and storage
rows.titleKo: Fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture 및 storage

columns.text: Own or control operations which use this power generation source
columns.textKo: Own 또는 control operations which use this 발전 source

guidance.text: This question only appears if ‘Electricity generation' is selected in 1.16.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 ‘Electricity generation' is 선택 in 1.16.

guidance.text:
General 
Report nameplate capacity by primary power generation sources owned or controlled by your organization.
Report your preferred measure (gross or net) of generation. Although CDP encourages disclosure of both, reporting one measure is sufficient.
Refer to the CDP Technical note on Biofuels for guidance on biomass/biofuel sustainability.
Power generation source (column 0) 
See guidance for specific power generation sources below. The examples are illustrative and are not meant to be exhaustive:
"Coal - Hard": report coal generation using anthracite and/or bituminous coal.
"Coal - Brown": report coal generation using sub-bituminous coal and/or lignite.
"Sustainable biomass" and
"Other biomass" : biomass may be combusted on its own or co-fired with other fuels. Provide aggregated data for the biomass that you combust on its own and biomass that you co-combust with other fuel sources. 
"Waste (non-biomass)" : waste can include tire-derived fuels and other refuse-derived fuels. When reporting in this category, only report the nameplate capacity and gross electricity generation data from the non-biomass fraction. The biomass fraction should be reported under either biomass option. 
"Hydropower" : hydropower does not include pumped storage operations for the reporting of nameplate capacity and gross electricity generation. CDP regards it as a form of managing or storing energy rather than primary generation. Note you will be asked to report water accounting volumes for these operations. 
"
Wave ": wave energy can include technologies such as point absorbers, hinge rafts/attenuators, oscillating wave surge converters or water columns.
"
Tidal ": tidal technology can include energy generation from rise and fall tides, ebb and flood currents, as well as the use of horizontal or vertical-axis turbines.
If you are using hydrogen for power generation:
Report generation using renewably derived hydrogen in row 
"Other renewable". 
Report generation using hydrogen derived from fossil fuels in row 
"Other non-renewable". 
Report generation using hydrogen produced from fossil fuels using carbon capture and storage technology (i.e. blue hydrogen) in row 
"Fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture and storage" .
In all cases, provide the nameplate capacity and generation figures associated with this in the Comment field.
If part of your organization's power plant capacity is composed of multiple mixed small-scale generation technologies that are difficult to report by specific power generation source, then these can be aggregated by renewable and non-renewable sources. Report the total capacity of aggregated renewable sources under "Other renewable" and the total capacity of aggregated non-renewable sources under "Other non-renewable". You can explain the methodology utilized and list the small-scale technologies used in the comment section. 
Comment (column 5) (optional) 
Use this column to provide any additional relevant information.
If you report information in the "Sustainable biomass" row, provide the criteria used to classify the biomass as sustainable (e.g. certification).
If you report information in any of the biomass rows, specify if the biomass technology refers to bioenergy plants fitted with carbon capture and storage (BECCS).
guidance.textKo: 일반 Report nameplate capacity by primary 발전 sources owned 또는 controlled by 귀사. Report your preferred measure (gross 또는 net) of generation. Although CDP encourages 공시 of both, reporting one measure is sufficient. Refer to the CDP Technical note on Biofuels for guidance on biomass/biofuel sustainability. 발전 source (열 0) See guidance for specific 발전 sources below. The examples are illustrative 및 are not meant to be exhaustive: "Coal - Hard": report coal generation using anthracite 및/또는 bituminous coal. "Coal - B행n": report coal generation using sub-bituminous coal 및/또는 lignite. "Sustainable biomass" 및 "Other biomass": biomass may be combusted on its own 또는 co-fired with other fuels. Provide aggregated data 에 대한 biomass that you combust on its own 및 biomass that you co-combust with other fuel sources. "Waste (non-biomass)": waste can include tire-derived fuels 및 other refuse-derived fuels. 경우 reporting in this category, only report the nameplate capacity 및 gross electricity generation data from the non-biomass fraction. The biomass fraction 해야 합니다 be 보고 under either biomass option. "Hydropower": hydropower does not include pumped storage operations 에 대한 reporting of nameplate capacity 및 gross electricity generation. CDP regards it as a form of managing 또는 storing energy rather than primary generation. Note you will be asked to report water accounting volumes 에 대한se operations. " Wave ": wave energy can include technologies such as 점 absorbers, hinge rafts/attenuators, oscillating wave surge converters 또는 water 열. " Tidal ": tidal 기술 can include energy generation from rise 및 fall tides, ebb 및 flood currents, as well as the use of horizontal 또는 vertical-axis turbines. 만약 you are using hydrogen for 발전: Report generation using re신규ably derived hydrogen in 행 "Other re신규able". Report generation using hydrogen derived from fossil fuels in 행 "Other non-re신규able". Report generation using hydrogen produced from fossil fuels using carbon capture 및 storage 기술 (i.e. blue hydrogen) in 행 "Fossil-fuel plants fitted with carbon capture 및 storage". In all cases, provide the nameplate capacity 및 generation figures associated with this in the Comment field. 만약 part of 귀사의 power plant capacity is composed of multiple mixed small-scale generation technologies that are difficult to report by specific 발전 source, then these can be aggregated by re신규able 및 non-re신규able sources. Report the total capacity of aggregated re신규able sources under "Other re신규able" 및 the total capacity of aggregated non-re신규able sources under "Other non-re신규able". You can explain the 방법론 utilized 및 list the small-scale technologies used in the comment section. Comment (열 5) (optional) Use this 열 to provide 하나 이상 additional 관련 information. 만약 you report information in the "Sustainable biomass" 행, provide the 기준 used to classify the biomass as sustainable (e.g. certification). 만약 you report information in 하나 이상 의 biomass 행, specify 만약 the biomass 기술 refers to bioenergy plants fitted with carbon capture 및 storage (BECCS).

guidance.text:
Biomass: any organic matter, i.e. biological material, available on a renewable basis. Includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and industrial sources. Biomass fuels should be sustainably sourced and certified where possible, and include: 
Solid biofuels: solid fuels derived from biomass. Includes feedstock derived from animals or plants, such as wood and agricultural crops, and organic waste from municipal and industrial sources. 
Biogas: a mixture of methane (CH4) and carbon dioxide (CO2) used as fuel and produced by bacterial degradation of organic matter or through gasification of biomass. 
Liquid biofuels: liquid fuels derived from biomass such as ethanol and biodiesel. 
Carbon capture and storage (CCS): as defined by the IEA, a family of technologies and techniques that enable the capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) from fuel combustion or industrial processes, the transport of CO2 via shops or pipelines, and its storage underground, in depleted oil and gas fields and deep saline formations. 
Gross electricity generation: the total amount of electricity produced by a power generation plant before accounting for any internal consumption or losses within the plant itself. It is typically measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). 
Nameplate capacity: the maximum rated output of a generator, prime mover, or other electric power production equipment under specific conditions designated by the manufacturer. It is typically measured in megawatts (MW). 
Net electricity generation: the total electricity output from a power generation plant available for external use or distribution after deducting the electricity consumed internally for the plant's own operational needs. It is typically measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). 
Renewable energy : energy taken from the sources that are inexhaustible, e.g. wind, water, solar, geothermal energy and sustainable biofuels (adapted from GHG Protocol, 2004 ).
guidance.textKo: Biomass: 하나 이상 organic matter, i.e. biological material, available on a re신규able basis. Includes feedstock derived from animals 또는 plants, such as wood 및 agricultural crops, 및 organic waste from municipal 및 industrial sources. Biomass fuels 해야 합니다 be sustainably sourced 및 certified 여기서 possible, 및 include: Solid biofuels: solid fuels derived from biomass. Includes feedstock derived from animals 또는 plants, such as wood 및 agricultural crops, 및 organic waste from municipal 및 industrial sources. Biogas: a mixture of methane (CH4) 및 carbon dioxide (CO2) used as fuel 및 produced by bacterial degradation of organic matter 또는 through gasification of biomass. Liquid biofuels: liquid fuels derived from biomass such as ethanol 및 biodiesel. Carbon capture 및 storage (CCS): as defined by the IEA, a family of technologies 및 techniques that enable the capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) from fuel combustion 또는 industrial 프로세스es, the transport of CO2 via shops 또는 pipelines, 및 its storage underground, in depleted oil 및 gas fields 및 deep saline formations. Gross electricity generation: the total amount of electricity produced by a 발전 plant before accounting for 하나 이상 internal consumption 또는 losses within the plant itself. It is typically measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). Nameplate capacity: the 최고 rated output of a generator, prime mover, 또는 other electric power production equipment under specific conditions designated by the manufacturer. It is typically measured in megawatts (MW). Net electricity generation: the total electricity output from a 발전 plant available for external use 또는 distribution after deducting the electricity consumed internally 에 대한 plant's own operational needs. It is typically measured in gigawatt-hours (GWh). 재생에너지: energy taken from the sources that are inexhaustible, e.g. wind, water, solar, geothermal energy 및 sustainable biofuels (adapted from GHG Protocol, 2004).

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed (excluding both rows 'Other renewable' AND 'Other non-renewable'). Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
Either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR column 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' is completed in addition to all other columns.
A maximum of 8.5/8.5 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성 행 in proportion to the number of 행 공개 (제외 both 행 'Other re신규able' 그리고 'Other non-re신규able'). 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. Either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 열 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' is 완성 in addition to all other 열. A 최고 of 8.5/8.5 점 is available for this 문항.

criteria.criteria:
Full Disclosure points must be awarded to be eligible for Awareness points.
i) Figure in row 'Total' in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' excluding row 'Total'
OR
Figure in row 'Total' in column 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in column 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' excluding row 'Total' - 2 points
ii) Figure in row 'Total' in column 'Nameplate capacity (MW)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in column 'Nameplate capacity (MW)' excluding row 'Total' - 2 points
A maximum of 4/4 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 인지 점수를 획득하려면 공시에서 최고점을 획득해야 합니다. i) Figure in 행 'Total' in 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 제외 행 'Total' 또는 Figure in 행 'Total' in 열 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in 열 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' 제외 행 'Total' - 2 점 ii) Figure in 행 'Total' in 열 'Nameplate capacity (MW)' is equal to (± 5%) sum of figures in 열 'Nameplate capacity (MW)' 제외 행 'Total' - 2 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 4/4점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Full Awareness points must be awarded to be eligible for Management points.
ROUTE A)
Sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- 'Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- 'Onshore wind'
- 'Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- 'Wave'
- 'Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
...accounts for greater than 25% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 2 points
A maximum of 2/2 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE B)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'
- 'Geothermal'
- 'Ocean thermal'
- 'Hydropower'
- 'Onshore wind'
- 'Offshore wind'
- 'Solar'
- 'Wave'
- 'Tidal'
- 'Other renewable'
... accounts for between 10% and 25% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 1 point
A maximum of 1/2 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 관리 점수를 획득하려면 인지에서 최고점을 획득해야 합니다. 경로 A) Sum of figures 입력 in either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in 행 with the following options 선택 in 열 '발전 source'... - 'Sustainable biomass' - 'Nuclear' - 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture 및 Storage' - 'Geothermal' - 'Ocean thermal' - 'Hydropower' - 'Onshore wind' - 'Offshore wind' - 'Solar' - 'Wave' - 'Tidal' - 'Other re신규able'...accounts for 초과 25% 의 sum of figures 입력 in either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 2 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/2점입니다. 또는 경로 B) Sum of figures 입력 in 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in 행 with the following options 선택 in 열 '발전 source'... - 'Sustainable biomass' - 'Nuclear' - 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture 및 Storage' - 'Geothermal' - 'Ocean thermal' - 'Hydropower' - 'Onshore wind' - 'Offshore wind' - 'Solar' - 'Wave' - 'Tidal' - 'Other re신규able'... accounts for between 10% 및 25% 의 sum of figures 입력 in either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 1 점 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/2점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Full Management points must be awarded to be eligible for Leadership points.
ROUTE A)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'

...accounts for greater than 90% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 3 points.
A maximum of 3/3 points is available for this route.
OR

A maximum of 2/3 points is available for this route.
OR
ROUTE C)
Sum of figures provided in column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in rows with the following options selected in column 'Power generation source'...
- 'Sustainable biomass'
- 'Nuclear'
- 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture and Storage'

...accounts for greater than 50% of the sum of figures provided in either column 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' OR 'Net electricity generation (GWh) - 1/3 points.
A maximum of 1/3 points is available for this route.
criteria.criteriaKo: 리더십 점수를 획득하려면 관리에서 최고점을 획득해야 합니다. 경로 A) Sum of figures 입력 in 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in 행 with the following options 선택 in 열 '발전 source'... - 'Sustainable biomass' - 'Nuclear' - 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture 및 Storage' - 'Geothermal' - ‘Ocean thermal' - 'Hydropower' - ‘Onshore wind' - ‘Offshore wind' - 'Solar' - ‘Wave' - ‘Tidal' - 'Other re신규able'...accounts for 초과 90% 의 sum of figures 입력 in either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 3 점. 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 3/3점입니다. 또는 경로 B) Sum of figures 입력 in 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in 행 with the following options 선택 in 열 '발전 source'... - 'Sustainable biomass' - 'Nuclear' - 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture 및 Storage' - 'Geothermal' - ‘Ocean thermal' - 'Hydropower' - ‘Onshore wind' - ‘Offshore wind' - 'Solar' - ‘Wave' - ‘Tidal' - 'Other re신규able'...accounts for 초과 75% 의 sum of figures 입력 in either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' - 2/3 점. 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 2/3점입니다. 또는 경로 C) Sum of figures 입력 in 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh)' in 행 with the following options 선택 in 열 '발전 source'... - 'Sustainable biomass' - 'Nuclear' - 'Fossil-fuel plants fitted with Carbon Capture 및 Storage' - 'Geothermal' - ‘Ocean thermal' - 'Hydropower' - ‘Onshore wind' - ‘Offshore wind' - 'Solar' - ‘Wave' - ‘Tidal' - 'Other re신규able'...accounts for 초과 50% 의 sum of figures 입력 in either 열 'Gross electricity generation (GWh)' 또는 'Net electricity generation (GWh) - 1/3 점. 이 경로에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/3점입니다.

Update in accordance with questionnaire change.

Update in accordance with questionnaire change.
CC Energy utilities and power generators Leadership • ROUTE A updated to reflect additional power generation sources added to question; ‘Ocean thermal', ‘Onshore wind', ‘Offshore wind', ‘Wave & Tidal' are newly added while ‘Wind' & ‘Marine' have been removed. Update in accordance with questionnaire change.

simpleHelpKo: Each 발전 source is associated with specific dependencies, impacts, 리스크, 및 기회, requiring tailored 관리 절차s 및 future planning. For example, hydropower will use 및 impact water resources in a different way to biomass 발전 plants. Asking organizations to disclose their 발전 sources provides 데이터 사용자 with the contextual information necessary to understand 및 evaluate how they assess 및 manage climate- 및 water-related dependencies, impacts, 리스크, 및 기회. This also enables 데이터 사용자 to make more accurate cross-organizational 및 cross-industry comparisons.
